name: Garlic Mashed Potatoes
recipeIngredient: 2 Heads garlic (broken up but not peeled)
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel and cut the potatoes into quarters.
2. Place the potatoes in a cold, medium-size pan and bring to a boil. Cook for 15-to-20 minutes, or until tender.
3. While the potatoes are cooking, plunge the garlic into boiling water and cook for 5 minutes.
4. Drain the potatoes when they are cool enough to handle.
5. Place the garlic in a small sauté pan with half the butter over low heat for 10 minutes. Be very careful not to let the garlic burn.
6. Add 1 cup of milk, bring to a boil, and simmer for 2 minutes.
7. Puree the mixture in a blender or food mill, or press through a sieve.
8. Drain the potatoes well, then place them through a potato ricer or food mill, or use a potato masher (do not use a food processor as it will make the potatoes gummy).
9. If preparing ahead of time, reheat in an oven set to 325°F for 20-to-30 minutes.
10. Add more butter, one tablespoon at a time, warmed milk and seasonings to taste. Serve immediately for best flavor.
